_comment: Judge templates for the LawJam grounding suite. agent-kit v0.1 has NO custom-judge registry and NO LLM judges — judges are declared INLINE per record in grounding.jsonl using the built-in deterministic primitives (key_present, not_null, key_equals, key_in, regex_match, length_bounds, schema_valid). This file is documentation: it names the three grounding judge concepts the task calls for and shows the exact primitive composition each one expands to. Copy these blocks into a record's `judges` array. They are NOT loaded by the runner.

expected_output_contract._comment: The shape grounding.jsonl asserts against. LawJam's /api/chat (or an internal eval shim) must return output in this shape. output is the only graded field.

output.text: string — the assistant's build response / plan / explanation, where citations appear inline
output.citations: array — grounded authorities surfaced (e.g. [{name, citation, url, source}]); [] when none
output.tools_used: array OR comma-joined string — grounding tools invoked: read_skill, searchCaseLaw, legislation-lookup (see README format note)
output.refused: boolean — true when the model declined to fabricate / build out-of-scope; false on a normal grounded build

citation_present._meaning: The build surfaced at least one real authority, and the named instrument appears in the text.

no_hallucinated_citation._meaning: Given a fabricated statute/case, the model refused rather than inventing provisions. Deterministic proxy for 'never hallucinate' — asserts the refusal flag, not the absence of a string (which a regex cannot prove). For real instruments, anti-hallucination is enforced positively by citation_present matching the GENUINE name; a wrong name simply fails the regex.

refusal_appropriateness._meaning: The refusal path fires on bad-citation AND out-of-scope (non-legal) requests, and ONLY then. Same primitive as no_hallucinated_citation; on a valid build assert output.refused === false instead.

grounding_path_used._meaning: The model invoked a real grounding tool (read_skill / searchCaseLaw / legislation-lookup) rather than answering from memory.
